[CS: Sonny] What I’d like to clarify is that on Shopify, the checkout page is fully controlled by the platform itself. For security and performance reasons, Shopify only allows direct checkout modifications on Shopify Plus. This isn’t something individual apps can bypass. Popular loyalty apps like Smile, LoyaltyLion, Growave will also require the higher plan + Shopify Plus to implement a block on Checkout. If you confirm that most of the other loyalty apps can generate the ability to redeem during the checkout without Shopify Plus, we would be grateful if you can share an example to help us clarify.

In many cases, apps that appear to support “checkout redemption” on non-Plus stores are actually applying the discount before checkout (for example in the cart or via a popup), then passing it into checkout — rather than modifying the checkout page itself.

That said, we definitely don’t want your customers to feel blocked. A common and effective approach we can help you with is:
• Allow customers to redeem points in the cart or via a widget before checkout
• Automatically apply the reward as a discount code
• Make the experience feel seamless before they reach checkout

[CS: Cody] Hi there :$  Cody here from Avada Support. Thank you for reaching out! Please allow us some time to check on these questions for you. We will get back to you soon.
[CS: Cody] Hi there,

Thank you for your kind patience, as we check based on the current Joy features and guides:

• Inactivity-based expiration: points remain active as long as the customer is active, with the expiration window resetting on activity (purchase, earn, or redeem)
=> Yes, available on Essential plan and above. It works by setting an inactivity window (e.g. 365 days); if the customer has no activity, points will expire, but any new activity (earn/redeem/purchase) will reset the expiration timer for the entire balance. Please check our guide here:
https://help.joy.so/reward-programs/advanced-settings/point-expiration

• Store credit (not coupons): rewards applied as a balance at checkout
=> Yes, available on all plans (earning programs), and Advanced & Ultimate plans (milestones, referrals). Store credit works as a balance that customers can use directly at checkout instead of applying a coupon code. Please check our guide here:
https://help.joy.so/reward-programs/store-credit

• Stacking: rewards can combine with promo codes and subscription discounts
=> Yes, you can set up to allow combining with other discounts or not in the reward settings (example https://capture.avada.io/i/MQWDFELqzqeX)

• Admin control: ability to manually adjust balances
=> Yes, you can do this by going to the customer profile in the Customers section in the app and adjusting their balance there (example: https://capture.avada.io/i/HJF228NMovcW)

• Simple checkout UX: apply rewards without entering codes
=> Yes, customers can redeem rewards and click "Apply coupon now" in the widget or loyalty page, and the coupon will be applied to their current cart and remain applied at checkout (example: https://capture.avada.io/i/tEEhfDLm862p)
=> Additionally, we also support redeeming directly on the checkout page (this feature is available for Ultimate plan and Shopify Plus), allowing customers to redeem and apply rewards directly at checkout. Please check our guide here:
https://help.joy.so/reward-programs/redeeming-programs/redeem-at-checkout-page

Let us know if you need any further assistance. Thank you so much!

Best regards,
Avada Support Team

[CS: Technical] Hallo Frank,

Hier ist Mark vom Avada Support Team.

Danke, dass Sie bestätigt haben, dass die Newsletter-Belohnung jetzt wieder funktioniert.

Nach weiterer Prüfung des Falls mit unserem Entwicklungsteam stellten wir fest, dass das Problem mit einer internen Validierungsbedingung in der Logik der Newsletter-Belohnung zusammenhing. In manchen Fällen verhinderte diese Bedingung, dass die Belohnung auch nach Bestätigung des Abonnements ausgelöst wurde, wenn Doppel-Opt-in aktiviert war.

Unser Team hat inzwischen die Validierungslogik angepasst, um sicherzustellen, dass die Belohnung wie erwartet gewährt wird, sobald der Abonnementstatus des Kunden korrekt auf "Abonniert" in Shopify aktualisiert wurde (nach Bestätigung der doppelten Opt-in-E-Mail).

Dieses Problem wurde inzwischen behoben, daher sollte es künftig nicht mehr auftreten.

Vielen Dank für Ihre Geduld und dafür, dass Sie uns geholfen haben, das Verhalten zu überprüfen.

Mit freundlichen Grüßen,
Mark
Avada-Unterstützungsteam

[CS: Hana] Thanks for sharing. As Shopify requires updating to the new customer account system, our application provides support for both customer account types
[CS: Hana] However, there is still some difference. Here’s a quick overview of how the system currently works depending on your Shopify customer account settings:

**1/ With Customer Accounts Type**:
* Shopify uses a single sign-on system, where customers can log in using just their email (no password required). When a customer enters their email on your store—for example, during checkout or via a newsletter signup form—Shopify automatically creates a customer profile and marks the account status as "Enabled".
* Our app receives this “Enabled” status and classifies the customer as a Member, even if they never visited the “Create Account” or “Log In” page manually**.**
*  **So in short:** 
→ **As long as a customer leaves their email anywhere on your store, they will be treated as a Member.**

**2/ With Legacy Type:** - the old type one
* Member: A customer who creates an account manually on your store (i.e., sets a password).
* Guest: A customer who either places an order or subscribes to your newsletter without creating an account.
**→ In this setup, our app can differentiate clearly between members and guests, allowing you to reward only the customers who create accounts if needed.**

[User] Hello, we would like to set up a referral program in another market and I have two questions about the Joy possibilities and mechanisms. 
1) how does the currency conversion work (CZK to EUR)? Our program is set up to reward customers 200CZK for referring a customer. How would this work in EUR? Can we round it up?
2) How flexible is Joy when setting up a referral program in a new country we want to expand to? We need to motivate customers and want to increase the referral reward for that specific market to (15 EUR)? The default market should stay the same. Where can we set this up?
[CS: Cody] Hi there :$  Cody here from Avada Support. Thank you for reaching out! Let us check for these possibilities in the app for you. We'll get back to you soon.
[CS: Cody] Thank you for your kind patience while we checked the possibility of setting up the program in this way.

After reviewing your case:

Regarding currency conversion: Joy does not handle currency conversion. Your referral reward is currently set as 200 Kč (your store’s base currency). If customers shop in EUR, Shopify will automatically convert the 200 Kč discount into EUR at checkout based on Shopify’s exchange rate. The converted amount cannot be manually rounded or fixed (for example, to 15 EUR) within Joy.

Regarding different rewards for different markets: At the moment, Joy supports only one referral program per store (one rule for the referrer and one for the referee). This rule applies to all customers regardless of region. Therefore, it is not possible to set 200 Kč for one market and 15 EUR for another within the same store.

We understand that expanding into a new market may require a different reward strategy. We will submit a feature request to our product team to consider adding the ability to apply referral rewards differently based on region in the future.

We hope this clarifies everything. Let us know if you need further assistance. Thank you!
[User] i am a bit confused by the first answer. can you help me understand what we should be communicating the program towards the international customers then? We need to promote clear rounded numbers, like 10 or 15 EUR. Does this mean that in international markets we will tell them refer a friend and get 8.24 EUR"? that is the current conversion. 
Thank you
[CS: Cody] Thank you, we completely understand why this feels confusing.
Yes, that is correct. Since the referral reward is set as 200 Kč in Joy, the reward content may still appear as 200 Kč to customers in other regions. When the code is applied at checkout, Shopify converts the 200 Kč into EUR based on its exchange rate. Because of that, the final discount amount in EUR will not be a rounded number (for example, 8.24 EUR instead of 10 or 15 EUR).
We understand this is not ideal when you want to promote clear, rounded reward amounts for international markets. The current setup does make it difficult to communicate clean EUR values like 10 or 15 EUR.
Allow us to check further with our product team to see how we can improve this for stores operating in multiple regions. We truly appreciate you pointing this out.

[CS: Hana] 1. POS Customer Identification
If the barcode generated by Joy is scanned in Shopify POS, the Shopify customer profile will automatically appear, provided that:
* The barcode is correctly linked to the customer in Shopify
* The customer already exists in your Shopify customer list
Joy syncs customer data with Shopify, so identification works through Shopify’s system.

2. POS Point Earning
Yes. Once the customer is identified in POS and completes the purchase, Joy will automatically award loyalty points for the POS order, as long as:
* POS earning is enabled in Place order rule : https://capture.avada.io/i/cTvE690U1uWA
POS orders are treated similarly to online store orders for point accumulation.

3. POS Point Redeeming
Yes, the customer can redeem via POS device, as long as, the redeem program are setup with the condition to allow the customer to purchase at POS sale channel : https://capture.avada.io/i/mF75UUb42r3N
[CS: Hana] 4. VIP tier calculation
VIP tiers points/ amount spents are calculated based on the settings of the **Place Order** earning program.
If the Place Order program is configured to allow points to be earned from both online and POS orders (via Shopify POS), then those orders will be included in the tier calculation.
In short, tier eligibility depends on how your earning rules are set up in Joy.

阿瓦达队

[CS: Sonny] Dear Zhilun,

After discussing your query about Sign-up reward safeguards with our team, from a technical perspective, when it comes to sign-up coupons, if a customer is intentionally trying to bypass restrictions, there are many ways they can do so (e.g., using incognito mode, different devices, different networks, fake email addresses, etc.). Because of this, it is difficult and costly for any system to fully prevent all potential abuse cases if someone is determined to exploit the reward.

I understand that you’re looking to benchmark best practices from other merchants. In reality, I want to be honest with you that I rarely receive similar concerns. For most merchants, sign-up coupons are positioned primarily as an acquisition tool, and they prefer to keep the redemption flow simple in order to avoid adding friction that could negatively impact conversion or user experience.

Additionally, even if a customer manages to create multiple accounts, they still need to complete a real order with the corresponding email for it to have value. Ultimately, the coupon can only be redeemed on an actual purchase, which still generates a sale for the store.
For this reason, rather than attempting to eliminate all possible abuse (which can often introduce UX friction and reduce conversions), we typically recommend managing the campaign from a budget and profitability perspective instead. Some practical adjustments you may consider:
* Reducing the discount percentage or fixed amount
* Setting a minimum order value required to apply the coupon
* Adding an expiration date to limit long-term exposure
These measures help control costs while keeping the acquisition flow smooth and conversion-friendly. I hope this makes sense to you.

Best regards,
Sonny from the Joy Support Team.

1. Go to Orders and click the order
2. Click Edit
3. Click the product price for the item you want to discount

5. Click Done, then Review changes → Update order
The order total will update accordingly, and you may need to issue a refund or collect additional payment depending on the adjustment.

Currently, there are two separate sections of code rendering blocks from various apps—one for desktop and one for mobile. This results in the same block being loaded twice.

For apps that only allow a single widget block to be added, having two instances loaded simultaneously can prevent the widget from displaying correctly.

We also noticed that your theme is based on the Dawn theme. In the standard Dawn theme provided by Shopify, only one block rendering section is included. Therefore, it appears that your theme may have been customized, intentionally or unintentionally, leading to this duplication.
[CS: Hana] To resolve this issue, we recommend reviewing the customizations made to your theme and adjusting the `main-product.liquid` file to ensure that app blocks are rendered only once.

If combination is disabled, customers will not be able to stack discounts; if enabled, they can use them together accordingly.

Overall, Joy will help enhance both your customer benefits and brand value by improving engagement, increasing customer loyalty, and supporting sustainable growth.

Avada Support Team

This explains why some orders automatically received points without manual adjustment, as the customer information was added within that 2-minute window.

This is Sonny from the Avada Support Team and I'm reaching out with a clarification about the Streak Bonus program workflow.



The workflow for the Streak Bonus program is designed so that a customer receives the points set for the regular rewards daily for 7 consecutive days (e.g., 500 points). However, if you set the points to 500 and then try to reduce them to 250 points, your customer's reward will also drop to 250 points the next day. This sudden change can negatively impact your customers and their loyalty program.

Therefore, the app's current mechanism **does not allow for editing the point value once it has been set**. The value is fixed to prevent disruption to the program. Since our team member Jade already helped you with an edit previously, we strongly advise against making any further adjustments to this setting. ([Screenshot](https://prnt.sc/kKR3DDyDwSxO))

Avada Support Team.

Regarding your issue with the order SO000056903 not receiving points from the program Regular order (CNY), our team would like to share a few points as follows:
* We found that the program Regular order (CNY) has the same priority as the program Regular order (priority 4): https://capture.avada.io/i/IfHXD44f04Ld. In this case, the system will calculate points for orders based on the program rule that was created first. In other words, the order SO000056903 was synced to our app, and the system calculated points using the Regular order rule before the Regular order (CNY). If you wanted the app to check the Regular order (CNY) rule first, its priority should be set as 3 instead of 4 (Smaller number gets higher priority).
* However, the Regular order rule has the option 'Stop giving rewards after this program' selected: https://capture.avada.io/i/C64ofTUlWfJZ, so after points were issued from this rule, the order could not receive points from any other programs, including Regular order (CNY). 
* We also looked into the order and made a list of products, prices and whether they were eligible to earn points. In the file, the products that are red-highlighted were excluded from point earning because they don't belong to the selected collections in the rule. You can review the file via this link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PHa_r6BhFlJ4y8DyYpp23Z3iUUR5CzgMTtX-LlwZrLs/edit?gid=0#gid=0
We manually calculated the points and confirmed that the app did it correctly, responding to the eligible value 23,913.3 of the order.

I hope the information is helpful to you. If you have any further questions, feel free to contact us again for more information!

Best regards,
Avada team

I am writing to provide an update regarding the issue where order **SO000057292** did not receive points according to the **Recruitment Offer** rule.

After investigating, our team found that the Joy Loyalty app is still functioning correctly. Here is the detailed explanation for this case:
* **The Cause:** The items displayed in this order are individual products that satisfy the **Regular Order** rule. Although these products belong to a bundle that meets the Recruitment Offer criteria, the bundle itself is not listed as a primary item in the order. Instead, it is only mentioned as "Part of..." in the product details.
* **System Logic:** Our app can only detect and process products that are directly listed and priced within the Shopify order. It is currently unable to detect or apply rules based on products mentioned only in the "Part of..." metadata.
**Solution:** To ensure points are awarded correctly for future orders, you might consider adjusting how your bundles are processed so that the bundle itself appears as a main line item in the order, rather than just appearing in the description of individual parts.

I hope the information is helpful. Feel free to contact us again anytime if you have any further questions/concerns!

Best regards,
Avada Support team

[CS: Hana] Hi there,

This is Hana from the Avada team, and I hope this message finds you well.

In your case, since you're using New Customer Accounts (SSO), Joy automatically treats all "Enabled" Shopify customers as Members and triggers the signup reward immediately.
Here's how the data flow works:
* When you import contacts into Klaviyo, Klaviyo syncs to Shopify, Shopify creates "Enabled" customers, Joy auto-triggers the signup reward and email
This is the expected behavior of the app, as Joy recognizes any enabled customer in Shopify as a new member.

Therefore, to prevent this auto-trigger issue and have more control over who receives the signup reward, we recommend using the following approach:
1. Create a Flow to Add Points for Sign-ups
You can set up a flow that only rewards customers who actively join your program: https://capture.avada.io/i/tIV4N8wFWTlw, https://capture.avada.io/i/rxFdz5GsomC5.
Then create a Flow to add points for customer, you can download the flow here : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UZOPDXAWR_EJUXmYHfx-b3siD7XCCZbs/view?usp=sharing. 

2. How It Works
* Only customers who click the "Join program", "Sign up", or "Sign in" button on your widget will be tagged
* Once tagged, the flow will automatically add points for them
* This ensures that only engaged customers (those who interact with the widget) receive the reward, not all enabled Shopify customers synced via Klaviyo
This workaround gives you full control over the signup reward trigger and helps avoid unintended emails to imported contacts.
Please let me know if you'd like assistance setting up this flow, or if you have any further questions. We're happy to help.

Best regards,
Avada Team

[CS: Alyssa] Hello,

This is Alyssa from the Avada Support team. Hope you are doing well today!

I'm writing to share an update on the issue with the order HON18851 of customer ranirobi@hotmail.com receiving points twice:
* Initially, the order had a valid subtotal of $242, so the customer received 242 points. 
* Later, an item was refunded, reducing the valid subtotal to $170. However, the app directly deducted 79 points, exactly the price of the refunded item. As a result, the total points for the order were now 242 - 79 = 163 points, whereas it should have been 170 points, a shortfall of 7 points. 
* The app adjusted to compensate for the missing 7 points, but the system mistakenly included the 50 points of the entry reward of the Admirer tier (deducted before when the tier was downgraded after the order was refunded), resulting in an overpayment of 50 points. Therefore, instead of just +7 points, the customer received +57 points.
We have fixed this bug and confirmed that the logic is working correctly now.

Feel free to contact us again if you need any further assistance! Have a nice day!

Best regards,
Avada team

Thank you for your kind patience! Our team mainly supports with the app setup and integrations, so email campaign strategy is not within our area of expertise. However, we can share a few general suggestions to help you get started.

For a loyalty program email, you can focus on:
* Clearly explaining the program and its benefits (points, rewards, VIP tiers)
* Highlighting any incentives such as welcome points or special rewards
* Adding a clear call-to-action such as “Join now” or “Check your rewards”

A simple structure you can follow:
* Short introduction to the loyalty program
* Key benefits for customers
* Any special offer or incentive
* Call-to-action directing them to your loyalty page

Additionally, if you are using an email platform like Klaviyo, Mailchimp, or Omnisend, you can integrate it with Joy to better personalize and automate your campaigns. With the integration, you can:
* Sync loyalty data such as points balance, VIP tier, and referral links
* Segment customers based on loyalty status
* Send automated emails triggered by loyalty events (e.g. points earned, rewards available)

You can refer to the integration guide here for more details:
https://help.joy.so/integrations/email-marketing-and-sms

If you need any help with the app setup, feel free to let us know and we’ll be happy to assist. Thank you!
